title: Jamie Dimon throws caution on the AI frenzy but sees promise in space data centers
excerpt: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has his doubts that the AI spending boom will turn out the way investors are hoping for. Still, he's willing to entertain one...
content_html:
JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon has his doubts that the AI spending boom will turn out the way investors are hoping for. Still, he's willing to entertain one of the AI build-out's more extraordinary projects: SpaceX's ambition to put data centers in space. 

"When I look at AI itself, the amount of money being spent is huge. Will it, in total, pay off? Probably, just like the internet did. Will it pay off the way you expect in the timetable you expect? Definitely not," Dimon said during a recent interview on the Master Investor Podcast with Wilfred Frost.

When asked if the market is pricing in a perfect outcome of the AI boom, Dimon said, "Not perfect, but probably a good outcome." 

Dimon added that companies may control spending on generative AI usage and seek out cheaper ways of using the technology, which "will cause some of the issues out there." Recently, the release of another cheap Chinese AI model, Kimi K3, has caused a stir in the US tech industry.

Dimon is far from the only Wall Street chief to throw caution on the AI gold rush.

"It won't be without bumps and recalibrations," Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon said of the AI boom during the bank's earnings call last week.

During the same podcast interview, Dimon described rocket maker SpaceX (SPCX) — one of the companies at the center of the spending boom — as "extraordinary."

He praised the company's satellite internet business and defended the feasibility of one of its more far-flung AI plays: space data centers.

"I've seen numbers on the data centers in space that can actually work … if it works, you're talking about very cheap energy, cheap cooling, very stable," Dimon said. 

JPMorgan Chase reported the highest quarterly profit in US banking history last week, with major windfalls coming from AI-related deals and stock trading. The bank earned hundreds of millions of dollars in fees advising on SpaceX's monster IPO and other mega deals tied to AI. 

Dimon interviewed Musk as part of the rocket maker's investor roadshow ahead of its public listing. JPMorgan also hosted over a hundred SpaceX employees and executives at its midtown Manhattan headquarters for the company's post-IPO listing party on June 12.

SpaceX's stock rose in its first three days of trading but has since tumbled, reaching a new low on Monday before regaining ground on Tuesday. The stock is down more than 5% from its initial listing price.

When asked how much he thought about SpaceX's listing price, Dimon said "quite a bit." In the IPO process, SpaceX upended the standard Wall Street process for establishing a price range by calling for a flat price of $135 per share.

content_html_zh:
摩根大通 (JPM) 首席执行官杰米·戴蒙 (Jamie Dimon) 对人工智能支出热潮能否如投资者所希望的那样表示怀疑。 尽管如此，他还是愿意接受人工智能建设中更非凡的项目之一：SpaceX 将数据中心放入太空的雄心。

“当我审视人工智能本身时，所花费的资金是巨大的。 总的来说，它会得到回报吗？ 也许，就像互联网一样。 它会在您期望的时间表内以您期望的方式获得回报吗？ 绝对不是，”戴蒙最近在投资大师播客上接受威尔弗雷德·弗罗斯特采访时说道。

当被问及市场是否正在消化人工智能热潮的完美结果时，戴蒙表示：“并不完美，但可能是一个好的结果。”

戴蒙补充说，公司可能会控制生成式人工智能使用的支出，并寻求更便宜的使用该技术的方式，这“将导致一些问题”。 近日，中国另一款廉价人工智能机型Kimi K3的发布，在美国科技界引起轰动。

戴蒙并不是唯一一位对人工智能淘金热持谨慎态度的华尔街高管。

高盛首席执行官大卫·所罗门在上周该行的财报电话会议上谈到人工智能的繁荣时表示：“这不会没有障碍和重新调整。”

在同一个播客采访中，戴蒙形容火箭制造商 SpaceX (SPCX)——处于支出热潮中心的公司之一——是“非凡的”。

他赞扬了该公司的卫星互联网业务，并为其更广泛的人工智能业务之一：太空数据中心的可行性进行了辩护。

“我看到了太空数据中心的实际运行数据…… 如果它有效，那么你谈论的是非常便宜的能源、便宜的冷却、非常稳定，”戴蒙说。

摩根大通上周公布了美国银行业历史上最高的季度利润，其中大部分意外之财来自人工智能相关交易和股票交易。 该银行为 SpaceX 的大规模 IPO 和其他与人工智能相关的大型交易提供咨询服务，赚取了数亿美元的费用。

作为火箭制造商公开上市前投资者路演的一部分，戴蒙采访了马斯克。 6 月 12 日，摩根大通还在曼哈顿中城总部接待了 100 多名 SpaceX 员工和高管，参加了该公司 IPO 后的上市派对。

SpaceX 的股价在前三天交易中上涨，但此后暴跌，周一触及新低，然后于周二收复失地。 该股较最初上市价下跌逾 5%。

当被问及他对 SpaceX 的上市价格有多少看法时，戴蒙说“相当多”。 在IPO过程中，SpaceX颠覆了华尔街确定价格范围的标准流程，要求统一定价为每股135美元。
